name: Apollo Global Management Leveraged Buyout Agreement
description: Leveraged buyout of Rackspace Hosting, Inc. (now Rackspace Technology Global, Inc.) by Inception Parent, Inc., an Apollo Global Management investment vehicle, completed November 3, 2016 for approximately $4.3 billion; transaction took the company private until re-IPO in August 2020. Created multi-tiered entity structure with Inception Parent and Inception Intermediate as Apollo acquisition vehicles. Debt structure persists to present (as of Mar 2024 refinancing), now $1.3B term loan. Apollo retained 53-57% post-IPO ownership (unusual for PE exit), creating hybrid public-private structure.

name: AWS Strategic Collaboration Agreement (SCA) — October 2024
description: Multi-year Strategic Collaboration Agreement (SCA) signed between Rackspace Technology and Amazon Web Services in October 2024. Covers advisory services, application and infrastructure migration, modernization, data, machine learning, and generative AI solutions across Rackspace's full customer base. Deepens the AWS-Rackspace partnership beyond reseller terms to include joint go-to-market, co-sell, and co-investment obligations. Rackspace cited this agreement as a catalyst for the Public Cloud segment growth strategy in FY2025 guidance. rackspace.com press release October 2024; Rackspace 10-K FY2024 Item 1.

provenance.primary_data_source: Rackspace Technology 10-K 2024, SEC filings, credit agreements

name: Rubrik Cyber Recovery Partnership and Reseller Agreement — 2024
description: Reseller and joint go-to-market agreement between Rackspace Technology and Rubrik, Inc. covering the Rackspace Cyber Recovery powered by Rubrik solution. Rackspace bundles Rubrik Enterprise Edition backup and Rubrik Security Cloud with its Immutable Recovery Environment (IRE) to deliver Cyber Recovery as a managed service offering. Solution targets ransomware recovery use cases with air-gapped, immutable backups and SLA-backed recovery times. Available to Rackspace managed cloud customers across AWS and private cloud segments.

name: Apollo Global Management Portfolio Governance Framework
description: Ongoing governance and reporting framework established under Apollo Global Management's leveraged buyout of Rackspace Technology (completed November 2016, per ctr-015). Apollo holds approximately 58% of Rackspace's outstanding shares as of FY2024. Framework governs board representation rights (Apollo nominates majority of board seats), information rights, anti-dilution protections, registration rights, and reporting obligations to Apollo affiliates. Rackspace is required to provide Apollo with non-public financial information under the governance framework.

Broadcom subscription licensing transition creates material cost risk disclosed in Rackspace 10-K. Agreement governs Rackspace Private Cloud powered by VMware product line.

name: First Lien Credit Agreement (March 2024 Refinancing)
description: $1.3B senior secured term loan facility maturing May 15, 2028. Closed March 12, 2024 under distressed exchange (72% lender, 64% noteholder participation). Borrower: Rackspace Finance, LLC. Interest rate: SOFR + 2.75%. Administrative Agent: Citibank N.A. Contains restrictive negative covenants over asset sales, debt incurrence, dividends, M&A, and change-of-control. Springing financial covenant: 5.0x net senior secured leverage ratio if revolver utilization >35%. Eliminates $375M net debt, raises $275M new money. Make-whole premium until September 12, 2025. Change-of-control mandatory offer to repurchase or lender consent required.", "properties": {"contract_type": "Credit Facility", "contract_status": "Active", "total_contract_value": 1300000000, "currency": "USD", "start_date": "2024-03-12", "end_date": "2028-05-15", "jurisdiction": "Delaware", "parties": ["Rackspace Finance, LLC", "Citibank N.A.", "Syndicated Lenders"]}

name: Broadcom VMware Subscription License Agreement - Post-Acquisition Pricing
description: VMware Enterprise License Agreement post-Broadcom acquisition (November 2023). Broadcom increased VMware licensing costs 200-300% through transition to subscription-only licensing model and elimination of perpetual licenses. Primary impact on Rackspace Private Cloud segment which licenses VMware vSphere, vSAN, NSX, and vCenter for managed private cloud infrastructure. Estimated annual cost increase $100-210M on current licensing footprint. Represents existential cost shock to Private Cloud segment economics. Agreement governs 60-70% of $1,055M Private Cloud revenue (VMware-based SDDC solutions estimated $630-740M). Rackspace has limited mitigation: (1) ABSORB pricing creating 10-20 point margin compression from 38.6% to 19-29% gross margin, destroying Private Cloud profitability; (2) PASS THROUGH pricing at 20-40% customer increase triggering 20-30% customer churn acceleration; (3) MIGRATE customers off VMware requiring 18-24 months per customer, 20-30% failure rate, customer consent requirement. Three impossible choices reveal structural pricing power asymmetry: Broadcom (supplier) controls pricing unilaterally, Rackspace (customer) has no leverage, Rackspace customers (Rackspace's customers) can switch to hyperscaler-native platforms. Cost shock timing: Hit Q1-Q2 2025 (partial impact on current contracts), full impact Q3-Q4 2025 (renewal contracts at higher rates). Margin crisis likely Q1-Q2 2026 when full cost impact reflected in financial results.

name: Email Hosting Platform Price Increase - March 2026 Effective
description: Email hosting pricing increase effective March 1, 2026: $3/mailbox/month increased to $10/mailbox/month (706% increase). Strategic intent: Improve margins on low-margin SMB customer segment (estimated segment revenue $135-275M, 5-10% of total). CONTRADICTS economic reality: Price increase triggers IMMEDIATE customer churn per partner reports ('immediate churn', 'wave of churn', 'devastating impact'). Baseline SMB email segment has ZERO lock-in across all dimensions: (1) Contractual lock-in ZERO - month-to-month billing, (2) Technical lock-in ZERO - email migration 1-5 days, (3) Competitive alternatives ABUNDANT - Microsoft 365 Business Basic $6/month with Office suite, Google Workspace, (4) Switching cost $0-5K typically covered by new provider free migration. Price elasticity of demand: HIGHLY ELASTIC in SMB segment. Price increase 706% will trigger churn >60% within 6-12 months. Platform economics: Fixed costs estimated $50-80M annually (servers, storage, platform software, staff). Revenue base after 60-70% churn: $40-110M remaining revenue. Platform becomes LOSS-MAKING with segment operating loss estimated $10-40M annually. Options: (1) Exit segment - execute orderly customer migrations, subsidize transitions to Microsoft/Google, shut down platform (~$5-15M cost); (2) Revert pricing - damage brand and margins (too late, customers already departing); (3) Accept losses - unsustainable (segment becomes financial liability). Email pricing increase DEMONSTRATES ZERO PRICING POWER across organization - increases intended to fix economics ACCELERATE customer departure FASTER than cost savings realized. Pattern repeats: high-margin Private Cloud has VMware cost shock creating impossible choice to absorb/pass-through/migrate.
